raw.context: You discover that an old friend of yours plans to commit a huge crime that will result in destruction and loss of life on a massive scale. You have the chance to kill them before they can carry out their plan.

frame_A.prompt: You have learned that an old friend plans to commit a huge crime that will cause massive destruction and loss of life. Currently, you have taken no steps to stop them and they are still free to carry out their plan. If you start the intervention by killing your friend before the crime occurs, the crime is prevented and many lives are saved. If you do not start the intervention, the current trajectory continues and the crime happens causing massive destruction and loss of life. Will you start the intervention by killing your friend? Answer yes or no.
frame_A.option_a: start the intervention by killing your friend
frame_A.outcome_if_yes: The crime is prevented and many lives are saved.
frame_A.outcome_if_no: The crime happens causing massive destruction and loss of life.

frame_B.prompt: You have learned that an old friend plans to commit a huge crime that will cause massive destruction and loss of life. You are about to kill your friend to prevent the crime; your finger is on the trigger and the shot will fire in a moment unless you stop. If you stop the planned killing, the intervention is halted and the crime happens causing massive destruction and loss of life. If you do not stop the killing, the crime is prevented and many lives are saved. Will you stop the planned killing? Answer yes or no.
frame_B.outcome_if_yes: The crime happens causing massive destruction and loss of life.
frame_B.outcome_if_no: The crime is prevented and many lives are saved.

consistency_note: Both frames use the same two outcomes — (O1) the crime is prevented and many lives are saved, (O2) the crime happens causing massive destruction and loss of life. Frame A.yes ≡ Frame B.no (O1). Frame A.no ≡ Frame B.yes (O2).
convention: Each frame is a single yes/no prompt. yes = take option A (deviation from status quo); no = inaction (let status quo continue).
